disk_size.title: Disk Size (GiB)
disk_size.description: Root disk size in GiB (max 1 PiB). Must be at least 20 GiB — control-plane nodes need room for the Talos image plus kube-apiserver, etcd, scheduler, controller-manager, CNI, and CoreDNS image pulls during bootstrap. Undersized root disks trigger kubelet image GC mid-install and etcd never comes up.

pool.title: ZFS Pool
pool.description: Top-level ZFS pool name (e.g., 'default', 'tank'). This must be a pool, not a dataset path — use dataset_prefix for nested datasets. Run 'zpool list' on TrueNAS to see available pools.

dataset_prefix.title: Dataset Prefix
dataset_prefix.description: Optional dataset path under the pool for organizing VM storage. Example: if your layout is default/previewk8, set pool='default' and dataset_prefix='previewk8'. VMs are created at <pool>/<prefix>/omni-vms/.

network_interface.title: Network Interface
network_interface.description: Host network interface for the VM NIC. Can be a bridge (e.g., br0, br100), VLAN interface (e.g., vlan100), or physical interface (e.g., enp5s0). Run 'midclt call vm.device.nic_attach_choices' on TrueNAS to see available options.

advertised_subnets.title: Advertised Subnets
advertised_subnets.description: Pin etcd/kubelet to specific subnets when multiple NICs are present. Comma-separated CIDRs (e.g., 192.168.100.0/24). Required for stable multi-NIC clusters; auto-detected for single-NIC VMs. Docs: https://github.com/bearbinary/omni-infra-provider-truenas/blob/main/docs/multihoming.md

storage_disk_size.title: Storage Disk (GiB)
storage_disk_size.description: Optional. Add a dedicated data disk for persistent storage (Longhorn). Control planes typically leave this at 0 or blank. Minimum size when set is 5 GiB. Install Longhorn via Helm after cluster provisioning. Docs: https://github.com/bearbinary/omni-infra-provider-truenas/blob/main/docs/storage.md

encrypted.title: Encrypted Disk
encrypted.description: Enable ZFS native encryption (AES-256-GCM) on the VM disk. A unique passphrase is auto-generated per zvol and stored as a ZFS user property for automatic unlock.

extensions.title: Extra Extensions
extensions.description: Additional Talos system extensions (e.g., siderolabs/nfs-utils for democratic-csi NFS mode, siderolabs/drbd). Defaults qemu-guest-agent, util-linux-tools, and iscsi-tools are always included.

additional_disks.title: Additional Disks
additional_disks.description: Extra data disks beyond the root disk. Each disk creates a separate zvol attached to the VM. Use for dedicated etcd disks (fast SSD pool), bulk data disks (HDD pool), or node-local storage (Longhorn).

pool.title: ZFS Pool
pool.description: ZFS pool for this disk (defaults to the primary pool)

dataset_prefix.title: Dataset Prefix
dataset_prefix.description: Dataset path under the pool for this disk (overrides the MachineClass-level dataset_prefix). Example: 'etcd-data' places the zvol at <pool>/etcd-data/omni-vms/...

encrypted.description: Enable ZFS native encryption on this disk

name.title: Volume Name
name.description: Talos UserVolumeConfig name — the disk is formatted and mounted at /var/mnt/<name> inside the guest. Defaults to data-N (1-indexed). Set to 'longhorn' to match Longhorn's defaultDataPath = /var/mnt/longhorn.

filesystem.title: Filesystem
filesystem.description: Filesystem for the emitted UserVolumeConfig. xfs (default) is recommended for Longhorn; ext4 is available as an alternative.

additional_nics.title: Additional NICs
additional_nics.description: Extra network interfaces for network segmentation. Primary NIC is always attached via network_interface.

network_interface.description: Bridge, VLAN, or physical interface (e.g., br200, vlan100, enp6s0)

mtu.description: MTU size for this NIC. Omit to use the host default (typically 1500). Set to 9000 for jumbo frames on storage networks. The host bridge/VLAN must also be configured for the same MTU.

dhcp.description: Run a DHCPv4 client on this NIC. Omit to use the default (true when no static addresses are set, false when addresses are supplied). Set true explicitly to run DHCP alongside static addresses.

items.description: IPv4 or IPv6 address in CIDR form, e.g., 10.20.0.5/24 or fd00::5/64

addresses.description: Static addresses assigned to this NIC. Leave empty for DHCP-only. The pattern catches 'forgot the /24' typos at MachineClass apply time rather than at provision time — the Go-side validator still runs net.ParseCIDR and rejects semantically-invalid values.

gateway.description: Optional default route advertised via this NIC (IP address, not CIDR). Only meaningful when addresses are set — DHCP-only NICs get their gateway from the DHCP server. The pattern is intentionally loose; the Go-side validator runs net.ParseIP plus unicast/family/on-link checks.
